step_by_step_thinking:
In considering the causes of brittle bone syndrome, option A correctly identifies improper synthesis of procollagen as a critical issue. Collagen serves as the fundamental building block for bones' strength and integrity. Any defect during its biosynthesis will result in weaker structures prone to fractures—a hallmark symptom of brittle bone disorders like osteogenesis imperfecta. While polymerization of collagen (Option B), increased osteoclastic activity (Option C), and increased fibroblastic activity (Option D) might play roles under different circumstances, none address the core genesis of defects found in these syndromes better than poor procollagen processing.
